plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core physician traits directly map to innovation leadership traits, reframing clinicians as already equipped to innovate.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing innovation as foreign to recognizing it as an extension of clinicianship through trait overlap and identity reframing.
plan_units.rationale: This is the conceptual opener for Part 3 and establishes the learner's identity bridge into innovation work.
source_refs: You do not need to learn
simply need to apply your existing clinical traits
plan_units.title: Physicianship = Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand how the clinical method maps directly onto design thinking so learners can see innovation as a familiar process.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From perceiving design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as a familiar clinical workflow through direct step-by-step mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This is a core translation mechanism in the source and carries high pedagogical weight because it makes innovation operationally familiar.
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis = Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain the concrete correspondences between history-taking, diagnosis, treatment planning, and the design thinking sequence.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From perceiving design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as a familiar clinical workflow through direct step-by-step mapping.
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly breaks the workflow into several mapped steps; isolating the mapping reduces cognitive load and supports comprehension.
source_refs: When you take a patient's history
synthesize the physical exam and labs
plan_units.title: Clinical-to-Design Step Mapping

plan_units.learning_objective: Reframe innovation work as applying existing clinical reasoning to broken workflows and systems rather than only to individual patients.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From perceiving design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as a familiar clinical workflow through direct step-by-step mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This is the payoff of the design-thinking bridge and completes the conceptual transfer from clinic to system innovation.
source_refs: You run this innovation cycle twenty times a day
changing the 'patient' from a human being
to a broken workflow or system
plan_units.title: Systems as the New Patient

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable formula centered on balancing vision with organizational realities.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as vague to understanding it as a structured internal-change formula through guided reading and organizational framing.
plan_units.rationale: The source requires this reading block before Slide 3 and uses it to frame stakeholder alignment and internal scaling.
source_refs: calculated approach to driving change from within
plan_units.title: Required Reading: The Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship and locate the clinician's primary arena for impact inside existing health systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating entrepreneurship with innovation to recognizing intrapreneurship as the clinician's most immediate arena for scale through contrast of where change happens.
plan_units.rationale: This comparison is central to the section's opportunity framing and helps learners situate where innovation happens.
source_refs: We often conflate innovation with Entrepreneurship
The most immediate impact you can make
improve it from the inside out
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Define intrapreneurship as creating and implementing solutions within an existing organization using entrepreneurial principles.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating entrepreneurship with innovation to recognizing intrapreneurship as the clinician's most immediate arena for scale through contrast of where change happens.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the formal definition before introducing the added difficulty of internal constraints.
source_refs: create, develop, and implement innovative solutions
plan_units.title: Define the Intrapreneur's Arena

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because change must occur within load-bearing institutional constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating entrepreneurship with innovation to recognizing intrapreneurship as the clinician's most immediate arena for scale through contrast of where change happens.
plan_units.rationale: The design brief presents a distinct explanatory beat about constraints, politics, and resources that benefits from explicit isolation.
source_refs: without destroying the existing, load-bearing institutional structures
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur's Maze

plan_units.learning_objective: Distinguish between an unvetted idea and an actionable opportunity vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability within a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual idea generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit criteria for actionability inside an organization.
plan_units.rationale: The source marks this as important and it is a foundational concept for the program's practical orientation.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
stop generating ideas, and start engineering actionable opportunities
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why solution-first thinking is insufficient until a concept is vetted for execution in organizational context.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual idea generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit criteria for actionability inside an organization.
plan_units.rationale: This isolates the first half of the distinction and prepares learners to understand opportunity criteria.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought or a suggestion
We should use AI to write notes
On its own, its value is zero
plan_units.title: Why Ideas Have Zero Value on Their Own

plan_units.learning_objective: Describe an opportunity as an idea tested for feasibility, viability, desirability, and path to execution within a specific health system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual idea generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit criteria for actionability inside an organization.
plan_units.rationale: This deepens the definition by separating the positive criteria that convert an idea into an opportunity.
source_refs: rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
It has a clear path to execution
plan_units.title: Opportunity = Vetted for Action

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing to transform a superficial solution idea into a root-cause-based operational opportunity.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual idea generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit criteria for actionability inside an organization.
plan_units.rationale: The worked example is the clearest application asset in the source and provides the operational model learners will need for later discussion work.
source_refs: buy iPads for all the nurses
Discharge times are delayed by an average of 3 hours
design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
plan_units.title: Worked Example: From iPads to a Real Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand first-principles thinking as breaking a broken workflow into fundamental truths before rebuilding a better system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching the status quo to rebuilding from fundamentals through deconstruction of workflows into first principles.
plan_units.rationale: This is a discrete innovation method in the source and supports the shift from superficial iteration to genuine redesign.
source_refs: Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: The Deconstruction: First Principles Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the section's three core takeaways: clinicians are already equipped, intrapreneurship is the arena, and opportunities are the goal.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From discrete concepts to an integrated innovation stance through concise recap of identity, arena, and goal.
plan_units.rationale: This is a gestalt recap slide whose value is the simultaneous whole, so it should remain a singleton.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify learner recall and understanding of key Part 3 concepts including idea vs opportunity, clinical method to design thinking, and intrapreneurship constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to verification through formative questions that test the section's core distinctions and mappings.
plan_units.rationale: The source includes a complete knowledge check set that serves as end-of-part formative assessment aligned to the major concepts taught.
source_refs: idea rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner needs the primary-source basis for Sandra Lam's structured framing of intrapreneurship so the reading block feels anchored rather than ornamental.
Learner would benefit from external validation examples showing clinical workflow redesign as a form of design thinking transfer.
Learner needs deeper real-world illustrations of organizational constraints that make intrapreneurship harder than entrepreneurship.

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded explanation of each shared trait—empathy, curiosity, analytical prowess, resiliency, and comfort with ambiguity—and how each functions in both patient care and innovation work.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The high-level overlap and identity reframe.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners explain in their own words how a familiar clinical trait transfers to innovation practice.

sections.narrative_intent: Give learners enough reflective depth to internalize that innovation does not require a new personality, only a new application domain for existing strengths.
sections.title: Clinician Traits as Innovation Assets

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A fuller walk-through of each mapped step with brief explanation of why history-taking aligns to empathize, diagnosis to define, and treatment selection to ideate/prototype.

exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to match clinical actions to design-thinking stages.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: changing the 'patient' from a human being
exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to reframe one broken workflow in their environment as a 'patient' entering the innovation cycle.

sections.narrative_intent: Offload the detailed mapping logic to the workbook so the slide can remain a glanceable bridge rather than a text-heavy explanation.
sections.title: Clinical Method to Design Thinking Crosswalk

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Guided reading prompts focused on formula structure, organizational reality, existing resources, and stakeholder alignment.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The requirement to read and the central framing claim.

exercises.prompt_intent: Direct learners to identify what makes intrapreneurship structured rather than personality-based.

sections.narrative_intent: Turn the required reading into an active processing aid that primes the learner for the intrapreneurship slides.
sections.title: Reading Guide: The Intrapreneurship Formula

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A fuller compare/contrast of entrepreneurship and intrapreneurship, including internal constraints such as legacy systems, budgets, compliance, and politics.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The core distinction and visual contrast.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners sort example innovation efforts into entrepreneurship or intrapreneurship based on organizational context.
Ask learners to explain why scale may be easier once internal alignment is achieved.

sections.narrative_intent: Provide the nuance that the deck only signals visually: the intrapreneur's challenge is not less innovation, but more constrained innovation.
sections.title: Where Innovation Happens: Inside vs Outside the System

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded criteria unpacking feasibility, viability, desirability, and clear path to execution, plus a guided explanation of why unvetted ideas start at zero value.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The top-line distinction between idea and opportunity.

exercises.prompt_intent: Check recall of the formal distinction between an idea and an opportunity.
Ask learners to revise a solution-first statement into an opportunity statement anchored in organizational context.

sections.narrative_intent: This section carries the definitional and evaluative depth that would overcrowd the slide but is essential for practical transfer.
sections.title: From Idea to Opportunity

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Stepwise breakdown of how the iPads example fails, how root-cause analysis changes the framing, and why the reframed opportunity is superior.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The before/after contrast and final opportunity statement.

exercises.prompt_intent: Require learners to diagnose what is flawed in a solution-first proposal.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Chief Complaint: Discharge times are delayed by an average of 3 hours
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners formulate a chief complaint, root cause, and opportunity for a workflow in their own setting.

sections.narrative_intent: Make the productive struggle explicit so learners can imitate the reasoning process, not just the finished answer.
sections.title: Worked Example: Reframing a Bad Start

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Detailed explanation of reasoning by analogy versus first-principles deconstruction, using the old-system/raw-blocks/new-system sequence as a written analytic scaffold.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Breaking it down to fundamental truths
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to break a familiar workflow into raw component truths rather than inherited steps.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Building a new system from the ground up
exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to sketch a rebuilt workflow from the same raw materials.

sections.narrative_intent: Support true method transfer by giving learners structured space to practice deconstruction and reconstruction.
sections.title: First Principles Workbook Walkthrough

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Answer rationales and brief remediation notes for each knowledge-check concept, especially idea vs opportunity, design-thinking mapping, stakeholder alignment, and root cause orientation.

sections.narrative_intent: Use the workbook to convert the knowledge check from simple scoring into formative feedback and concept reinforcement.
sections.title: Knowledge Check Review and Rationale

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: Part 3 is planned as a progression from identity reframing, to process translation, to organizational arena, to disciplined opportunity framing, then closes with first-principles redesign, summary, and knowledge check. Dense concepts were clustered where the source contained multiple explanatory beats with high pedagogical weight: design thinking mapping, intrapreneurship contrast, and idea-versus-opportunity with worked example. Gestalt recap and comparison slides that derive value from simultaneous whole-view were kept dense as singletons. All units are sourced only from the provided corpus.

plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as vague to understanding it as a structured internal-change formula through guided reading and organizational framing.
plan_units.rationale: The source requires this reading block before Slide 3 and uses it to frame stakeholder alignment and internal scaling.
source_refs: calculated approach to driving change from within
plan_units.title: Required Reading: The Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship and locate the clinician's primary arena for impact inside existing health systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating entrepreneurship with innovation to recognizing intrapreneurship as the clinician's most immediate arena for scale through contrast of where change happens.
plan_units.rationale: This comparison is central to the section's opportunity framing and helps learners situate where innovation happens.
source_refs: We often conflate innovation with Entrepreneurship
The most immediate impact you can make
improve it from the inside out
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Define intrapreneurship as creating and implementing solutions within an existing organization using entrepreneurial principles.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating entrepreneurship with innovation to recognizing intrapreneurship as the clinician's most immediate arena for scale through contrast of where change happens.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the formal definition before introducing the added difficulty of internal constraints.
source_refs: create, develop, and implement innovative solutions
plan_units.title: Define the Intrapreneur's Arena

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because change must occur within load-bearing institutional constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating entrepreneurship with innovation to recognizing intrapreneurship as the clinician's most immediate arena for scale through contrast of where change happens.
plan_units.rationale: The design brief presents a distinct explanatory beat about constraints, politics, and resources that benefits from explicit isolation.
source_refs: without destroying the existing, load-bearing institutional structures
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur's Maze

plan_units.learning_objective: Distinguish between an unvetted idea and an actionable opportunity vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability within a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual idea generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit criteria for actionability inside an organization.
plan_units.rationale: The source marks this as important and it is a foundational concept for the program's practical orientation.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
stop generating ideas, and start engineering actionable opportunities
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why solution-first thinking is insufficient until a concept is vetted for execution in organizational context.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual idea generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit criteria for actionability inside an organization.
plan_units.rationale: This isolates the first half of the distinction and prepares learners to understand opportunity criteria.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought or a suggestion
We should use AI to write notes
On its own, its value is zero
plan_units.title: Why Ideas Have Zero Value on Their Own

plan_units.learning_objective: Describe an opportunity as an idea tested for feasibility, viability, desirability, and path to execution within a specific health system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual idea generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit criteria for actionability inside an organization.
plan_units.rationale: This deepens the definition by separating the positive criteria that convert an idea into an opportunity.
source_refs: rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
It has a clear path to execution
plan_units.title: Opportunity = Vetted for Action

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing to transform a superficial solution idea into a root-cause-based operational opportunity.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual idea generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit criteria for actionability inside an organization.
plan_units.rationale: The worked example is the clearest application asset in the source and provides the operational model learners will need for later discussion work.
source_refs: buy iPads for all the nurses
Discharge times are delayed by an average of 3 hours
design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
plan_units.title: Worked Example: From iPads to a Real Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand first-principles thinking as breaking a broken workflow into fundamental truths before rebuilding a better system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching the status quo to rebuilding from fundamentals through deconstruction of workflows into first principles.
plan_units.rationale: This is a discrete innovation method in the source and supports the shift from superficial iteration to genuine redesign.
source_refs: Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: The Deconstruction: First Principles Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the section's three core takeaways: clinicians are already equipped, intrapreneurship is the arena, and opportunities are the goal.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From discrete concepts to an integrated innovation stance through concise recap of identity, arena, and goal.
plan_units.rationale: This is a gestalt recap slide whose value is the simultaneous whole, so it should remain a singleton.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify learner recall and understanding of key Part 3 concepts including idea vs opportunity, clinical method to design thinking, and intrapreneurship constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to verification through formative questions that test the section's core distinctions and mappings.
plan_units.rationale: The source includes a complete knowledge check set that serves as end-of-part formative assessment aligned to the major concepts taught.
source_refs: idea rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
fall in love with the problem
plan_units.title: Knowledge Check
